w:r.w:t: ES sites detecting poliovirus within 14 days
Days from case confirmation to response campaign
% high-risk districts covered in first round
Vaccine stock mismatch vs target population
No unified dashboard at national level
List major mobile corridors, IDP routes, seasonal movements, and informal settlements.
Border districts regularly report AFP cases with no >60
day silent periods (relevant to surveillance indicators).
Specimen transport from border areas meets ≤72
border alerts or virus detections are shared promptly with neighboring countries.
Permanent or temporary vaccination posts exist at key land crossings.
Transit, nomadic, and mobile populations in border zones are included in
Vaccinator teams at borders have reliable vaccine stock and cold chain capacity.
Border vaccination data is reconciled with neighboring country data before/after SIAs.
Border areas assessed as High/Moderate/Fragile readiness for campaign operations.
Security risks at border could disrupt vaccination or supervision.
Logistic constraints (fuel, transport, access) may delay campaign rounds.
Health authorities participate in joint cross
with neighboring countries include synchronized campaign dates.
Border health officials have clear communication pathways for AFP alerts.
District and national EOCs integrate cross
Mobile corridors and informal settlements along borders are mapped.
dose or persistently missed children identified in border districts.
Daily campaign monitoring includes border and transit points.
Cross border teams (permanent transit teams, surveillance)
Polio plusses (blankets, soaps, other incentive items)
Share of government financing for vaccination
Data shared across partners in real time
List current methods to obtain population estimates for campaigns
